revision.text:
{{Short description|Founder of the Kawachi Genji)}} [[Image:Minamoto Yorinobu.jpg|thumb|250px|Minamoto no Yorinobu]] {{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} {{Nihongo|'''Minamoto no Yorinobu'''|源 頼信||December 21, 968 – June 1, 1048}} was a [[samurai]] commander and member of the powerful [[Minamoto clan]]. Along with his brother [[:en:Minamoto no Yorimitsu|Yorimitsu]], Yorinobu served the regents of the [[Fujiwara clan]], taking the violent measures the Fujiwara were themselves unable to take. He held the title, passed down from his father, of ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'', Commander-in-chief of the Defense of the North. He served as Governor of [[:en:Ise Province|Ise]], Shinano, Sagami, Mutsu and [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]]s, and was the progenitor of the [[:en:Kawachi Genji|Kawachi Genji]].
== Biography == [[File:Minamotono Yorinobu.jpg|thumb|Minamoto no Yorinobu approaches the residence of Taira no Tadatsune through the inland sea.]] Yorinobu was born on December 21, 968, the son of [[:en:Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Minamoto no Mitsunaka]] (912–997).
Yorinobu is particularly known for being a favorite of the [[Sessho and Kampaku|Regent]] [[:en:Fujiwara no Michinaga|Fujiwara no Michinaga]], and for suppressing a revolt by [[Taira no Tadatsune]], vice-governor of [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]]. He had originally refused, for personal reasons, but eventually assented to taking on the charge of defeating Tadatsune, after being made Governor of Kai. While making preparations to attack, Tadatsune surrendered, offering no resistance in 1031. In putting an end to this rebellion, Yorinobu not only furthered the Court's goals and his own reputation, but he created an opening for [[Minamoto clan|Minamoto]] influence in the eastern portions of the country.<ref name=Sansom>{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A history of Japan to 1334 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1958 |ISBN=0804705232 |page=247-248}}</ref>
In a statement to Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine, it was stated that the reason they were able to put down the rebellion so successfully was due to the blessing of inheriting the blood of [[Hachiman]] Daibosatsu and [[:en:Emperor Ojin|Emperor Ojin]].
The story of the capture of Tadatsune while Yorinobu was the Governor of Kai, and other stories while he was the governor of [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]] and [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]], appear in the [[:en:Konjaku Monogatarishū|Konjaku Monogatarishū]] (vol. 25, sec. 9 and 11).<ref name=Sato>{{Cite book |last=Sato |first=Hiroaki |title=Legends of the Samurai |publisher=Overlook Duckworth |year=1995 |ISBN=9781590207307 |page=71-79}}</ref>
After this rebellion, the warriors of Bando (the Taira clan, the Fujiwara clan of the Hidesato lineage, etc.) began to form vassal relationships with the Kawachi Genji, laying the foundation for their later domination of the eastern provinces and for the mainstreaming of the Minamoto clan as a warrior family.
His eldest son, Minamoto no Yoriyoshi, married Taira no Naokata's daughter and inherited the power of the direct line of the Bando Taira clan who gave birth to his famous grandson [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]]. He was a part of Michinaga’s Four Heavenly Kings along with Fujiwara no Yasumasa, Taira no Korehira and Taira no Muneyori.
The tachi Tenkomaru(天光丸) is said to have been passed down from Tada Mitsunaka to Minamoto no Yorinobu and Minamoto no Yoshiie as a treasured sword of the Genji clan.
His [[:en:Dharma name|Dharma name]] was '''Renshin''' (蓮心). He died on June 1, 1048.
==Family== * Father: ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'' [[:en:Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Minamoto no Mitsunaka]] * Mother: daughter of Fujiwara no Munetada * Wife: Shuri no Myobu * Children: [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * Grandchildren: [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] ** [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna]] ***[[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]]
== See also == * [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] * [[Seiwa Genji]] * [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * [[Hachiman]] * [[:en:Kidōmaru|Kidōmaru]]
==References== {{reflist}} *Sansom, George (1958). 'A History of Japan to 1334'. Stanford, California: Stanford University Press.

==Family== * Father: ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'' [[:en:Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Minamoto no Mitsunaka]] * Mother: daughter of Fujiwara no Munetada * Wife: Shuri no Myobu * Children: [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * Grandchildren: [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] ** [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna]] ***[[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]]

revision.comment: Bot: prepend Wikidata AFC comment via pages.txt
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q2699951]] Wikidata name: Minamoto no Yorinobu [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Founder of the Kawachi Genji)}} [[Image:Minamoto Yorinobu.jpg|thumb|250px|Minamoto no Yorinobu]] {{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} {{Nihongo|'''Minamoto no Yorinobu'''|源 頼信||December 21, 968 – June 1, 1048}} was a [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]] commander and member of the powerful [[Minamoto clan]]. Along with his brother [[:en:Minamoto no Yorimitsu|Yorimitsu]], Yorinobu served the regents of the [[Fujiwara clan]], taking the violent measures the Fujiwara were themselves unable to take. He held the title, passed down from his father, of ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'', Commander-in-chief of the Defense of the North. He served as Governor of [[:en:Ise Province|Ise]], Shinano, Sagami, Mutsu and [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]]s, and was the progenitor of the [[:en:Kawachi Genji|Kawachi Genji]].
== Biography == [[File:Minamotono Yorinobu.jpg|thumb|Minamoto no Yorinobu approaches the residence of Taira no Tadatsune through the inland sea.]] Yorinobu was born on December 21, 968, the son of [[:en:Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Minamoto no Mitsunaka]] (912–997).
Yorinobu is particularly known for being a favorite of the [[:en:Sessho and Kampaku|Regent]] [[:en:Fujiwara no Michinaga|Fujiwara no Michinaga]], and for suppressing a revolt by [[:en:Taira no Tadatsune|Taira no Tadatsune]], vice-governor of [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]]. He had originally refused, for personal reasons, but eventually assented to taking on the charge of defeating Tadatsune, after being made Governor of Kai. While making preparations to attack, Tadatsune surrendered, offering no resistance in 1031. In putting an end to this rebellion, Yorinobu not only furthered the Court's goals and his own reputation, but he created an opening for [[Minamoto clan|Minamoto]] influence in the eastern portions of the country.<ref name=Sansom>{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A history of Japan to 1334 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1958 |ISBN=0804705232 |page=247-248}}</ref>
In a statement to Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine, it was stated that the reason they were able to put down the rebellion so successfully was due to the blessing of inheriting the blood of [[Hachiman]] Daibosatsu and [[:en:Emperor Ojin|Emperor Ojin]].
The story of the capture of Tadatsune while Yorinobu was the Governor of Kai, and other stories while he was the governor of [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]] and [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]], appear in the [[:en:Konjaku Monogatarishū|Konjaku Monogatarishū]] (vol. 25, sec. 9 and 11).<ref name=Sato>{{Cite book |last=Sato |first=Hiroaki |title=Legends of the Samurai |publisher=Overlook Duckworth |year=1995 |ISBN=9781590207307 |page=71-79}}</ref>
After this rebellion, the warriors of Bando (the Taira clan, the Fujiwara clan of the Hidesato lineage, etc.) began to form vassal relationships with the Kawachi Genji, laying the foundation for their later domination of the eastern provinces and for the mainstreaming of the Minamoto clan as a warrior family.
His eldest son, Minamoto no Yoriyoshi, married Taira no Naokata's daughter and inherited the power of the direct line of the Bando Taira clan who gave birth to his famous grandson [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]]. He was a part of Michinaga’s Four Heavenly Kings along with Fujiwara no Yasumasa, Taira no Korehira and Taira no Muneyori.
The tachi Tenkomaru(天光丸) is said to have been passed down from Tada Mitsunaka to Minamoto no Yorinobu and Minamoto no Yoshiie as a treasured sword of the Genji clan.
His [[:en:Dharma name|Dharma name]] was '''Renshin''' (蓮心). He died on June 1, 1048.
==Family== * Father: ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'' [[:en:Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Minamoto no Mitsunaka]] * Mother: daughter of Fujiwara no Munetada * Wife: Shuri no Myobu * Children: [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * Grandchildren: [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] ** [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna]] ***[[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]]
== See also == * [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] * [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] * [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * [[Hachiman]] * [[:en:Kidōmaru|Kidōmaru]]
==References== {{reflist}} *Sansom, George (1958). 'A History of Japan to 1334'. Stanford, California: Stanford University Press.

The tachi Tenkomaru(天光丸) is said to have been passed down from Tada Mitsunaka to Minamoto no Yorinobu and Minamoto no Yoshiie as a treasured sword of the Genji clan.
His [[:en:Dharma name|Dharma name]] was '''Renshin''' (蓮心). He died on June 1, 1048.
==Family== * Father: ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'' [[:en:Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Minamoto no Mitsunaka]] * Mother: daughter of Fujiwara no Munetada * Wife: Shuri no Myobu * Children: [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * Grandchildren: [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] ** [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna]] ***[[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]]
== See also == * [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] * [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] * [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * [[Hachiman]] * [[:en:Kidōmaru|Kidōmaru]]
==References== {{reflist}} *Sansom, George (1958). 'A History of Japan to 1334'. Stanford, California: Stanford University Press.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[fa:میناموتو نو یورینوبو]][[fr:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[it:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[ja:源頼信]][[ko:미나모토노 요리노부]][[pt:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[ru:Минамото-но Ёринобу]][[sv:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[uk:Мінамото но Йорінобу]][[zh:源賴信]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Minamoto Yorinobu.jpg|birth_date={{birth date|0968|12|21}}|death_date={{death date|1048|6|1}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|military personnel|qid=Q47064}}; {{ill|samurai|qid=Q38142}}|children={{ill|Minamoto no Yorikiyo|qid=Q11564357}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yorinobu's daughter|qid=Q106533657}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yorisue|qid=Q11564346}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|qid=Q106533719}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|qid=Q3082851}}; {{ill|Q106619424|qid=Q106619424}}|father={{ill|Minamoto no Mitsunaka|qid=Q2531241}}|mother={{ill|Fujiwara no Motokata's daughter (Minamoto no Mitsunaka's wife)|qid=Q106326720}}; {{ill|Fujiwara no Munetada's daughter|qid=Q106326646}}|native_name=源頼信|given_name={{ill|Yorinobu|qid=Q8055328}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q2699951]] Wikidata name: Minamoto no Yorinobu [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Founder of the Kawachi Genji)}} [[Image:Minamoto Yorinobu.jpg|thumb|250px|Minamoto no Yorinobu]] {{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} {{Nihongo|'''Minamoto no Yorinobu'''|源 頼信||December 21, 968 – June 1, 1048}} was a [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]] commander and member of the powerful [[Minamoto clan]]. Along with his brother [[:en:Minamoto no Yorimitsu|Yorimitsu]], Yorinobu served the regents of the [[Fujiwara clan]], taking the violent measures the Fujiwara were themselves unable to take. He held the title, passed down from his father, of ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'', Commander-in-chief of the Defense of the North. He served as Governor of [[:en:Ise Province|Ise]], Shinano, Sagami, Mutsu and [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]]s, and was the progenitor of the [[:en:Kawachi Genji|Kawachi Genji]].
== Biography == [[File:Minamotono Yorinobu.jpg|thumb|Minamoto no Yorinobu approaches the residence of Taira no Tadatsune through the inland sea.]] Yorinobu was born on December 21, 968, the son of [[:en:Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Minamoto no Mitsunaka]] (912–997).
Yorinobu is particularly known for being a favorite of the [[:en:Sessho and Kampaku|Regent]] [[:en:Fujiwara no Michinaga|Fujiwara no Michinaga]], and for suppressing a revolt by [[:en:Taira no Tadatsune|Taira no Tadatsune]], vice-governor of [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]]. He had originally refused, for personal reasons, but eventually assented to taking on the charge of defeating Tadatsune, after being made Governor of Kai. While making preparations to attack, Tadatsune surrendered, offering no resistance in 1031. In putting an end to this rebellion, Yorinobu not only furthered the Court's goals and his own reputation, but he created an opening for [[Minamoto clan|Minamoto]] influence in the eastern portions of the country.<ref name=Sansom>{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A history of Japan to 1334 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1958 |ISBN=0804705232 |page=247-248}}</ref>
In a statement to Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine, it was stated that the reason they were able to put down the rebellion so successfully was due to the blessing of inheriting the blood of [[Hachiman]] Daibosatsu and [[:en:Emperor Ojin|Emperor Ojin]].
The story of the capture of Tadatsune while Yorinobu was the Governor of Kai, and other stories while he was the governor of [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]] and [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]], appear in the [[:en:Konjaku Monogatarishū|Konjaku Monogatarishū]] (vol. 25, sec. 9 and 11).<ref name=Sato>{{Cite book |last=Sato |first=Hiroaki |title=Legends of the Samurai |publisher=Overlook Duckworth |year=1995 |ISBN=9781590207307 |page=71-79}}</ref>
After this rebellion, the warriors of Bando (the Taira clan, the Fujiwara clan of the Hidesato lineage, etc.) began to form vassal relationships with the Kawachi Genji, laying the foundation for their later domination of the eastern provinces and for the mainstreaming of the Minamoto clan as a warrior family.
His eldest son, Minamoto no Yoriyoshi, married Taira no Naokata's daughter and inherited the power of the direct line of the Bando Taira clan who gave birth to his famous grandson [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]]. He was a part of Michinaga’s Four Heavenly Kings along with Fujiwara no Yasumasa, Taira no Korehira and Taira no Muneyori.
The tachi Tenkomaru(天光丸) is said to have been passed down from Tada Mitsunaka to Minamoto no Yorinobu and Minamoto no Yoshiie as a treasured sword of the Genji clan.
His [[:en:Dharma name|Dharma name]] was '''Renshin''' (蓮心). He died on June 1, 1048.
==Family== * Father: ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'' [[:en:Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Minamoto no Mitsunaka]] * Mother: daughter of Fujiwara no Munetada * Wife: Shuri no Myobu * Children: [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * Grandchildren: [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] ** [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna]] ***[[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]]
== See also == * [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] * [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] * [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * [[Hachiman]] * [[:en:Kidōmaru|Kidōmaru]]
==References== {{reflist}} *Sansom, George (1958). 'A History of Japan to 1334'. Stanford, California: Stanford University Press.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[fa:میناموتو نو یورینوبو]][[fr:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[it:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[ja:源頼信]][[ko:미나모토노 요리노부]][[pt:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[ru:Минамото-но Ёринобу]][[sv:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[uk:Мінамото но Йорінобу]][[zh:源賴信]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Minamoto Yorinobu.jpg|birth_date={{birth date|0968|12|21}}|death_date={{death date|1048|6|1}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|military personnel|WD=Q47064}}; {{ill|samurai|WD=Q38142}}|children={{ill|Minamoto no Yorikiyo|WD=Q11564357}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yorinobu's daughter|WD=Q106533657}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yorisue|WD=Q11564346}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q106533719}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|WD=Q3082851}}; {{ill|Q106619424|WD=Q106619424}}|father={{ill|Minamoto no Mitsunaka|WD=Q2531241}}|mother={{ill|Fujiwara no Motokata's daughter (Minamoto no Mitsunaka's wife)|WD=Q106326720}}; {{ill|Fujiwara no Munetada's daughter|WD=Q106326646}}|native_name=源頼信|given_name={{ill|Yorinobu|WD=Q8055328}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2699951]] Wikidata name: Minamoto no Yorinobu [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Founder of the Kawachi Genji)}} [[Image:Minamoto Yorinobu.jpg|thumb|250px|Minamoto no Yorinobu]] {{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} {{Nihongo|'''Minamoto no Yorinobu'''|源 頼信||December 21, 968 – June 1, 1048}} was a [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]] commander and member of the powerful [[Minamoto clan]]. Along with his brother [[:en:Minamoto no Yorimitsu|Yorimitsu]], Yorinobu served the regents of the [[Fujiwara clan]], taking the violent measures the Fujiwara were themselves unable to take. He held the title, passed down from his father, of ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'', Commander-in-chief of the Defense of the North. He served as Governor of [[:en:Ise Province|Ise]], Shinano, Sagami, Mutsu and [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]]s, and was the progenitor of the [[:en:Kawachi Genji|Kawachi Genji]].
== Biography == [[File:Minamotono Yorinobu.jpg|thumb|Minamoto no Yorinobu approaches the residence of Taira no Tadatsune through the inland sea.]] Yorinobu was born on December 21, 968, the son of [[:en:Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Minamoto no Mitsunaka]] (912–997).
Yorinobu is particularly known for being a favorite of the [[:en:Sessho and Kampaku|Regent]] [[:en:Fujiwara no Michinaga|Fujiwara no Michinaga]], and for suppressing a revolt by [[:en:Taira no Tadatsune|Taira no Tadatsune]], vice-governor of [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]]. He had originally refused, for personal reasons, but eventually assented to taking on the charge of defeating Tadatsune, after being made Governor of Kai. While making preparations to attack, Tadatsune surrendered, offering no resistance in 1031. In putting an end to this rebellion, Yorinobu not only furthered the Court's goals and his own reputation, but he created an opening for [[Minamoto clan|Minamoto]] influence in the eastern portions of the country.<ref name=Sansom>{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A history of Japan to 1334 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1958 |ISBN=0804705232 |page=247-248}}</ref>
In a statement to Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine, it was stated that the reason they were able to put down the rebellion so successfully was due to the blessing of inheriting the blood of [[Hachiman]] Daibosatsu and [[:en:Emperor Ojin|Emperor Ojin]].
The story of the capture of Tadatsune while Yorinobu was the Governor of Kai, and other stories while he was the governor of [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]] and [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]], appear in the [[:en:Konjaku Monogatarishū|Konjaku Monogatarishū]] (vol. 25, sec. 9 and 11).<ref name=Sato>{{Cite book |last=Sato |first=Hiroaki |title=Legends of the Samurai |publisher=Overlook Duckworth |year=1995 |ISBN=9781590207307 |page=71-79}}</ref>
After this rebellion, the warriors of Bando (the Taira clan, the Fujiwara clan of the Hidesato lineage, etc.) began to form vassal relationships with the Kawachi Genji, laying the foundation for their later domination of the eastern provinces and for the mainstreaming of the Minamoto clan as a warrior family.
His eldest son, Minamoto no Yoriyoshi, married Taira no Naokata's daughter and inherited the power of the direct line of the Bando Taira clan who gave birth to his famous grandson [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]]. He was a part of Michinaga’s Four Heavenly Kings along with Fujiwara no Yasumasa, Taira no Korehira and Taira no Muneyori.
The tachi Tenkomaru(天光丸) is said to have been passed down from Tada Mitsunaka to Minamoto no Yorinobu and Minamoto no Yoshiie as a treasured sword of the Genji clan.
His [[:en:Dharma name|Dharma name]] was '''Renshin''' (蓮心). He died on June 1, 1048.
==Family== * Father: ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'' [[:en:Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Minamoto no Mitsunaka]] * Mother: daughter of Fujiwara no Munetada * Wife: Shuri no Myobu * Children: [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * Grandchildren: [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] ** [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna]] ***[[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]]
== See also == * [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] * [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] * [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * [[Hachiman]] * [[:en:Kidōmaru|Kidōmaru]]
==References== {{reflist}} *Sansom, George (1958). 'A History of Japan to 1334'. Stanford, California: Stanford University Press.

He was a part of Michinaga’s Four Heavenly Kings along with Fujiwara no Yasumasa, Taira no Korehira and Taira no Muneyori.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[fa:میناموتو نو یورینوبو]][[fr:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[it:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[ja:源頼信]][[ko:미나모토노 요리노부]][[pt:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[ru:Минамото-но Ёринобу]][[sv:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[uk:Мінамото но Йорінобу]][[zh:源賴信]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Minamoto Yorinobu.jpg|birth_date={{birth date|0968|12|21}}|death_date={{death date|1048|6|1}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|military personnel|WD=Q47064}}; {{ill|samurai|WD=Q38142}}|children={{ill|Minamoto no Yorikiyo|WD=Q11564357}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yorinobu's daughter|WD=Q106533657}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yorisue|WD=Q11564346}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q106533719}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|WD=Q3082851}}; {{ill|Q106619424|WD=Q106619424}}|father={{ill|Minamoto no Mitsunaka|WD=Q2531241}}|mother={{ill|Fujiwara no Motokata's daughter (Minamoto no Mitsunaka's wife)|WD=Q106326720}}; {{ill|Fujiwara no Munetada's daughter|WD=Q106326646}}|native_name=源頼信|given_name={{ill|Yorinobu|WD=Q8055328}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2699951]] Wikidata name: Minamoto no Yorinobu [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Founder of the Kawachi Genji)}} [[Image:Minamoto Yorinobu.jpg|thumb|250px|Minamoto no Yorinobu]] {{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} {{Nihongo|'''Minamoto no Yorinobu'''|源 頼信||December 21, 968 – June 1, 1048}} was a [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]] commander and member of the powerful [[Minamoto clan]]. Along with his brother [[:en:Minamoto no Yorimitsu|Yorimitsu]], Yorinobu served the regents of the [[Fujiwara clan]], taking the violent measures the Fujiwara were themselves unable to take. He held the title, passed down from his father, of ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'', Commander-in-chief of the Defense of the North. He served as Governor of [[:en:Ise Province|Ise]], Shinano, Sagami, Mutsu and [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]]s, and was the progenitor of the [[:en:Kawachi Genji|Kawachi Genji]].

He had originally refused, for personal reasons, but eventually assented to taking on the charge of defeating Tadatsune, after being made Governor of Kai. While making preparations to attack, Tadatsune surrendered, offering no resistance in 1031. In putting an end to this rebellion, Yorinobu not only furthered the Court's goals and his own reputation, but he created an opening for [[Minamoto clan|Minamoto]] influence in the eastern portions of the country.<ref name=Sansom>{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A history of Japan to 1334 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1958 |ISBN=0804705232 |page=247-248}}</ref>

His [[:en:Dharma name|Dharma name]] was '''Renshin''' (蓮心). He died on June 1, 1048.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[fa:میناموتو نو یورینوبو]][[fr:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[it:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[ja:源頼信]][[ko:미나모토노 요리노부]][[pt:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[ru:Минамото-но Ёринобу]][[sv:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[uk:Мінамото но Йорінобу]][[zh:源賴信]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Minamoto Yorinobu.jpg|birth_date={{birth date|0968|12|21}}|death_date={{death date|1048|6|1}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|military personnel|WD=Q47064}}; {{ill|samurai|WD=Q38142}}|children={{ill|Minamoto no Yorikiyo|WD=Q11564357}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yorinobu's daughter|WD=Q106533657}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yorisue|WD=Q11564346}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q106533719}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|WD=Q3082851}}; {{ill|Q106619424|WD=Q106619424}}|father={{ill|Minamoto no Mitsunaka|WD=Q2531241}}|mother={{ill|Fujiwara no Motokata's daughter (Minamoto no Mitsunaka's wife)|WD=Q106326720}}; {{ill|Fujiwara no Munetada's daughter|WD=Q106326646}}|native_name=源頼信|given_name={{ill|Yorinobu|WD=Q8055328}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2699951]] Wikidata name: Minamoto no Yorinobu [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Founder of the Kawachi Genji)}} [[Image:Minamoto Yorinobu.jpg|thumb|250px|Minamoto no Yorinobu]] {{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} {{Nihongo|'''Minamoto no Yorinobu'''|源 頼信||December 21, 968 – June 1, 1048}} was a [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]] commander and member of the powerful [[Minamoto clan]]. Along with his brother [[:en:Minamoto no Yorimitsu|Yorimitsu]], Yorinobu served the regents of the [[Fujiwara clan]], taking the violent measures the Fujiwara were themselves unable to take. He held the title, passed down from his father, of ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'', Commander-in-chief of the Defense of the North. He served as Governor of [[:en:Ise Province|Ise]], Shinano, Sagami, Mutsu and [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]]s, and was the progenitor of the [[:en:Kawachi Genji|Kawachi Genji]].
== Biography == [[File:Minamotono Yorinobu.jpg|thumb|Minamoto no Yorinobu approaches the residence of Taira no Tadatsune through the inland sea.]] Yorinobu was born on December 21, 968, the son of [[:en:Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Minamoto no Mitsunaka]] (912–997).
Yorinobu is particularly known for being a favorite of the [[:en:Sessho and Kampaku|Regent]] [[:en:Fujiwara no Michinaga|Fujiwara no Michinaga]], and for suppressing a revolt by [[:en:Taira no Tadatsune|Taira no Tadatsune]], vice-governor of [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]]. He had originally refused, for personal reasons, but eventually assented to taking on the charge of defeating Tadatsune, after being made Governor of Kai. While making preparations to attack, Tadatsune surrendered, offering no resistance in 1031. In putting an end to this rebellion, Yorinobu not only furthered the Court's goals and his own reputation, but he created an opening for [[Minamoto clan|Minamoto]] influence in the eastern portions of the country.<ref name=Sansom>{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A history of Japan to 1334 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1958 |ISBN=0804705232 |page=247-248}}</ref>
In a statement to Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine, it was stated that the reason they were able to put down the rebellion so successfully was due to the blessing of inheriting the blood of [[Hachiman]] Daibosatsu and [[:en:Emperor Ojin|Emperor Ojin]].
The story of the capture of Tadatsune while Yorinobu was the Governor of Kai, and other stories while he was the governor of [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]] and [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]], appear in the [[:en:Konjaku Monogatarishū|Konjaku Monogatarishū]] (vol. 25, sec. 9 and 11).<ref name=Sato>{{Cite book |last=Sato |first=Hiroaki |title=Legends of the Samurai |publisher=Overlook Duckworth |year=1995 |ISBN=9781590207307 |page=71-79}}</ref>
After this rebellion, the warriors of Bando (the Taira clan, the Fujiwara clan of the Hidesato lineage, etc.) began to form vassal relationships with the Kawachi Genji, laying the foundation for their later domination of the eastern provinces and for the mainstreaming of the Minamoto clan as a warrior family.
His eldest son, Minamoto no Yoriyoshi, married Taira no Naokata's daughter and inherited the power of the direct line of the Bando Taira clan who gave birth to his famous grandson [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]]. He was a part of Michinaga’s Four Heavenly Kings along with Fujiwara no Yasumasa, Taira no Korehira and Taira no Muneyori.
The tachi Tenkomaru(天光丸) is said to have been passed down from Tada Mitsunaka to Minamoto no Yorinobu and Minamoto no Yoshiie as a treasured sword of the Genji clan.
His [[:en:Dharma name|Dharma name]] was '''Renshin''' (蓮心). He died on June 1, 1048.
==Family== * Father: ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'' [[:en:Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Minamoto no Mitsunaka]] * Mother: daughter of Fujiwara no Munetada * Wife: Shuri no Myobu * Children: [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * Grandchildren: [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] ** [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna]] ***[[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]]
== See also == * [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] * [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] * [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * [[Hachiman]] * [[:en:Kidōmaru|Kidōmaru]]
==References== {{reflist}} *Sansom, George (1958). 'A History of Japan to 1334'. Stanford, California: Stanford University Press.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[fa:میناموتو نو یورینوبو]][[fr:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[it:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[ja:源頼信]][[ko:미나모토노 요리노부]][[pt:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[ru:Минамото-но Ёринобу]][[sv:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[uk:Мінамото но Йорінобу]][[zh:源賴信]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Minamoto Yorinobu.jpg|birth_date={{birth date|0968|12|21}}|death_date={{death date|1048|6|1}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|military personnel|WD=Q47064}}; {{ill|samurai|WD=Q38142}}|children={{ill|Minamoto no Yorikiyo|WD=Q11564357}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yorinobu's daughter|WD=Q106533657}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yorisue|WD=Q11564346}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q106533719}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|WD=Q3082851}}; {{ill|Q106619424|WD=Q106619424}}|father={{ill|Minamoto no Mitsunaka|WD=Q2531241}}|mother={{ill|Fujiwara no Motokata's daughter (Minamoto no Mitsunaka's wife)|WD=Q106326720}}; {{ill|Fujiwara no Munetada's daughter|WD=Q106326646}}|native_name=源頼信|given_name={{ill|Yorinobu|WD=Q8055328}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2699951]] Wikidata name: Minamoto no Yorinobu [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Founder of the Kawachi Genji)}} [[Image:Minamoto Yorinobu.jpg|thumb|250px|Minamoto no Yorinobu]] {{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} {{Nihongo|'''Minamoto no Yorinobu'''|源 頼信||December 21, 968 – June 1, 1048}} was a [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]] commander and member of the powerful [[Minamoto clan]]. Along with his brother [[:en:Minamoto no Yorimitsu|Yorimitsu]], Yorinobu served the regents of the [[Fujiwara clan]], taking the violent measures the Fujiwara were themselves unable to take. He held the title, passed down from his father, of ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'', Commander-in-chief of the Defense of the North. He served as Governor of [[:en:Ise Province|Ise]], Shinano, Sagami, Mutsu and [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]]s, and was the progenitor of the [[:en:Kawachi Genji|Kawachi Genji]].
== Biography == [[File:Minamotono Yorinobu.jpg|thumb|Minamoto no Yorinobu approaches the residence of Taira no Tadatsune through the inland sea.]] Yorinobu was born on December 21, 968, the son of [[:en:Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Minamoto no Mitsunaka]] (912–997).
Yorinobu is particularly known for being a favorite of the [[:en:Sessho and Kampaku|Regent]] [[:en:Fujiwara no Michinaga|Fujiwara no Michinaga]], and for suppressing a revolt by [[:en:Taira no Tadatsune|Taira no Tadatsune]], vice-governor of [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]]. He had originally refused, for personal reasons, but eventually assented to taking on the charge of defeating Tadatsune, after being made Governor of Kai. While making preparations to attack, Tadatsune surrendered, offering no resistance in 1031. In putting an end to this rebellion, Yorinobu not only furthered the Court's goals and his own reputation, but he created an opening for [[Minamoto clan|Minamoto]] influence in the eastern portions of the country.<ref name=Sansom>{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A history of Japan to 1334 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1958 |ISBN=0804705232 |page=247-248}}</ref>
In a statement to Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine, it was stated that the reason they were able to put down the rebellion so successfully was due to the blessing of inheriting the blood of [[Hachiman]] Daibosatsu and [[:en:Emperor Ojin|Emperor Ojin]].
The story of the capture of Tadatsune while Yorinobu was the Governor of Kai, and other stories while he was the governor of [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]] and [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]], appear in the [[:en:Konjaku Monogatarishū|Konjaku Monogatarishū]] (vol. 25, sec. 9 and 11).<ref name=Sato>{{Cite book |last=Sato |first=Hiroaki |title=Legends of the Samurai |publisher=Overlook Duckworth |year=1995 |ISBN=9781590207307 |page=71-79}}</ref>
After this rebellion, the warriors of Bando (the Taira clan, the Fujiwara clan of the Hidesato lineage, etc.) began to form vassal relationships with the Kawachi Genji, laying the foundation for their later domination of the eastern provinces and for the mainstreaming of the Minamoto clan as a warrior family.
His eldest son, Minamoto no Yoriyoshi, married Taira no Naokata's daughter and inherited the power of the direct line of the Bando Taira clan who gave birth to his famous grandson [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]]. He was a part of Michinaga’s Four Heavenly Kings along with Fujiwara no Yasumasa, Taira no Korehira and Taira no Muneyori.
The tachi Tenkomaru(天光丸) is said to have been passed down from Tada Mitsunaka to Minamoto no Yorinobu and Minamoto no Yoshiie as a treasured sword of the Genji clan.
His [[:en:Dharma name|Dharma name]] was '''Renshin''' (蓮心). He died on June 1, 1048.
==Family== * Father: ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'' [[:en:Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Minamoto no Mitsunaka]] * Mother: daughter of Fujiwara no Munetada * Wife: Shuri no Myobu * Children: [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * Grandchildren: [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] ** [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna]] ***[[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]]
== See also == * [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] * [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] * [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * [[Hachiman]] * [[:en:Kidōmaru|Kidōmaru]]
==References== {{reflist}} *Sansom, George (1958). 'A History of Japan to 1334'. Stanford, California: Stanford University Press.

Yorinobu is particularly known for being a favorite of the [[:en:Sessho and Kampaku|Regent]] [[:en:Fujiwara no Michinaga|Fujiwara no Michinaga]], and for suppressing a revolt by [[:en:Taira no Tadatsune|Taira no Tadatsune]], vice-governor of [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]]. He had originally refused, for personal reasons, but eventually assented to taking on the charge of defeating Tadatsune, after being made Governor of Kai. While making preparations to attack, Tadatsune surrendered, offering no resistance in 1031. In putting an end to this rebellion, Yorinobu not only furthered the Court's goals and his own reputation, but he created an opening for [[Minamoto clan|Minamoto]] influence in the eastern portions of the country.<ref name=Sansom>{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A history of Japan to 1334 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1958 |ISBN=0804705232 |page=247-248}}</ref>
In a statement to Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine, it was stated that the reason they were able to put down the rebellion so successfully was due to the blessing of inheriting the blood of [[Hachiman]] Daibosatsu and [[:en:Emperor Ojin|Emperor Ojin]].
The story of the capture of Tadatsune while Yorinobu was the Governor of Kai, and other stories while he was the governor of [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]] and [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]], appear in the [[:en:Konjaku Monogatarishū|Konjaku Monogatarishū]] (vol. 25, sec. 9 and 11).<ref name=Sato>{{Cite book |last=Sato |first=Hiroaki |title=Legends of the Samurai |publisher=Overlook Duckworth |year=1995 |ISBN=9781590207307 |page=71-79}}</ref>
After this rebellion, the warriors of Bando (the Taira clan, the Fujiwara clan of the Hidesato lineage, etc.) began to form vassal relationships with the Kawachi Genji, laying the foundation for their later domination of the eastern provinces and for the mainstreaming of the Minamoto clan as a warrior family.
His eldest son, Minamoto no Yoriyoshi, married Taira no Naokata's daughter and inherited the power of the direct line of the Bando Taira clan who gave birth to his famous grandson [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]]. He was a part of Michinaga’s Four Heavenly Kings along with Fujiwara no Yasumasa, Taira no Korehira and Taira no Muneyori.
The tachi Tenkomaru(天光丸) is said to have been passed down from Tada Mitsunaka to Minamoto no Yorinobu and Minamoto no Yoshiie as a treasured sword of the Genji clan.
His [[:en:Dharma name|Dharma name]] was '''Renshin''' (蓮心). He died on June 1, 1048.
==Family== * Father: ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'' [[:en:Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Minamoto no Mitsunaka]] * Mother: daughter of Fujiwara no Munetada * Wife: Shuri no Myobu * Children: [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * Grandchildren: [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] ** [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna]] ***[[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]]
== See also == * [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] * [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] * [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * [[Hachiman]] * [[:en:Kidōmaru|Kidōmaru]]
==References== {{reflist}} *Sansom, George (1958). 'A History of Japan to 1334'. Stanford, California: Stanford University Press.

Yorinobu is particularly known for being a favorite of the [[:en:Sessho and Kampaku|Regent]] [[:en:Fujiwara no Michinaga|Fujiwara no Michinaga]], and for suppressing a revolt by [[:en:Taira no Tadatsune|Taira no Tadatsune]], vice-governor of [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]]. He had originally refused, for personal reasons, but eventually assented to taking on the charge of defeating Tadatsune, after being made Governor of Kai. While making preparations to attack, Tadatsune surrendered, offering no resistance in 1031. In putting an end to this rebellion, Yorinobu not only furthered the Court's goals and his own reputation, but he created an opening for [[Minamoto clan|Minamoto]] influence in the eastern portions of the country.<ref name=Sansom>{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A history of Japan to 1334 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1958 |ISBN=0804705232 |page=247-248}}</ref>
In a statement to Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine, it was stated that the reason they were able to put down the rebellion so successfully was due to the blessing of inheriting the blood of [[Hachiman]] Daibosatsu and [[:en:Emperor Ojin|Emperor Ojin]].
The story of the capture of Tadatsune while Yorinobu was the Governor of Kai, and other stories while he was the governor of [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]] and [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]], appear in the [[:en:Konjaku Monogatarishū|Konjaku Monogatarishū]] (vol. 25, sec. 9 and 11).<ref name=Sato>{{Cite book |last=Sato |first=Hiroaki |title=Legends of the Samurai |publisher=Overlook Duckworth |year=1995 |ISBN=9781590207307 |page=71-79}}</ref>
After this rebellion, the warriors of Bando (the Taira clan, the Fujiwara clan of the Hidesato lineage, etc.) began to form vassal relationships with the Kawachi Genji, laying the foundation for their later domination of the eastern provinces and for the mainstreaming of the Minamoto clan as a warrior family.
His eldest son, Minamoto no Yoriyoshi, married Taira no Naokata's daughter and inherited the power of the direct line of the Bando Taira clan who gave birth to his famous grandson [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]]. He was a part of Michinaga’s Four Heavenly Kings along with Fujiwara no Yasumasa, Taira no Korehira and Taira no Muneyori.
The tachi Tenkomaru(天光丸) is said to have been passed down from Tada Mitsunaka to Minamoto no Yorinobu and Minamoto no Yoshiie as a treasured sword of the Genji clan.
His [[:en:Dharma name|Dharma name]] was '''Renshin''' (蓮心). He died on June 1, 1048.
==Family== * Father: ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'' [[:en:Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Minamoto no Mitsunaka]] * Mother: daughter of Fujiwara no Munetada * Wife: Shuri no Myobu * Children: [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * Grandchildren: [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] ** [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna]] ***[[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]]
== See also == * [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] * [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] * [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * [[Hachiman]] * [[:en:Kidōmaru|Kidōmaru]]
==References== {{reflist}} *Sansom, George (1958). 'A History of Japan to 1334'. Stanford, California: Stanford University Press.

Yorinobu is particularly known for being a favorite of the [[:en:Sessho and Kampaku|Regent]] [[:en:Fujiwara no Michinaga|Fujiwara no Michinaga]], and for suppressing a revolt by [[:en:Taira no Tadatsune|Taira no Tadatsune]], vice-governor of [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]]. He had originally refused, for personal reasons, but eventually assented to taking on the charge of defeating Tadatsune, after being made Governor of Kai. While making preparations to attack, Tadatsune surrendered, offering no resistance in 1031. In putting an end to this rebellion, Yorinobu not only furthered the Court's goals and his own reputation, but he created an opening for [[Minamoto clan|Minamoto]] influence in the eastern portions of the country.<ref name=Sansom>{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A history of Japan to 1334 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1958 |ISBN=0804705232 |page=247-248}}</ref>
In a statement to Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine, it was stated that the reason they were able to put down the rebellion so successfully was due to the blessing of inheriting the blood of [[Hachiman]] Daibosatsu and [[:en:Emperor Ojin|Emperor Ojin]].
The story of the capture of Tadatsune while Yorinobu was the Governor of Kai, and other stories while he was the governor of [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]] and [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]], appear in the [[:en:Konjaku Monogatarishū|Konjaku Monogatarishū]] (vol. 25, sec. 9 and 11).<ref name=Sato>{{Cite book |last=Sato |first=Hiroaki |title=Legends of the Samurai |publisher=Overlook Duckworth |year=1995 |ISBN=9781590207307 |page=71-79}}</ref>
After this rebellion, the warriors of Bando (the Taira clan, the Fujiwara clan of the Hidesato lineage, etc.) began to form vassal relationships with the Kawachi Genji, laying the foundation for their later domination of the eastern provinces and for the mainstreaming of the Minamoto clan as a warrior family.
His eldest son, Minamoto no Yoriyoshi, married Taira no Naokata's daughter and inherited the power of the direct line of the Bando Taira clan who gave birth to his famous grandson [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]]. He was a part of Michinaga’s Four Heavenly Kings along with Fujiwara no Yasumasa, Taira no Korehira and Taira no Muneyori.
The tachi Tenkomaru(天光丸) is said to have been passed down from Tada Mitsunaka to Minamoto no Yorinobu and Minamoto no Yoshiie as a treasured sword of the Genji clan.
His [[:en:Dharma name|Dharma name]] was '''Renshin''' (蓮心). He died on June 1, 1048.
==Family== * Father: ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'' [[:en:Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Minamoto no Mitsunaka]] * Mother: daughter of Fujiwara no Munetada * Wife: Shuri no Myobu * Children: [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * Grandchildren: [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] ** [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna]] ***[[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]]
== See also == * [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] * [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] * [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * [[Hachiman]] * [[:en:Kidōmaru|Kidōmaru]]
==References== {{reflist}} *Sansom, George (1958). 'A History of Japan to 1334'. Stanford, California: Stanford University Press.

Yorinobu is particularly known for being a favorite of the [[:en:Sessho and Kampaku|Regent]] [[:en:Fujiwara no Michinaga|Fujiwara no Michinaga]], and for suppressing a revolt by [[:en:Taira no Tadatsune|Taira no Tadatsune]], vice-governor of [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]]. He had originally refused, for personal reasons, but eventually assented to taking on the charge of defeating Tadatsune, after being made Governor of Kai. While making preparations to attack, Tadatsune surrendered, offering no resistance in 1031. In putting an end to this rebellion, Yorinobu not only furthered the Court's goals and his own reputation, but he created an opening for [[Minamoto clan|Minamoto]] influence in the eastern portions of the country.<ref name=Sansom>{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A history of Japan to 1334 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1958 |ISBN=0804705232 |page=247-248}}</ref>
In a statement to Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine, it was stated that the reason they were able to put down the rebellion so successfully was due to the blessing of inheriting the blood of [[Hachiman]] Daibosatsu and [[:en:Emperor Ojin|Emperor Ojin]].
The story of the capture of Tadatsune while Yorinobu was the Governor of Kai, and other stories while he was the governor of [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]] and [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]], appear in the [[:en:Konjaku Monogatarishū|Konjaku Monogatarishū]] (vol. 25, sec. 9 and 11).<ref name=Sato>{{Cite book |last=Sato |first=Hiroaki |title=Legends of the Samurai |publisher=Overlook Duckworth |year=1995 |ISBN=9781590207307 |page=71-79}}</ref>
After this rebellion, the warriors of Bando (the Taira clan, the Fujiwara clan of the Hidesato lineage, etc.) began to form vassal relationships with the Kawachi Genji, laying the foundation for their later domination of the eastern provinces and for the mainstreaming of the Minamoto clan as a warrior family.
His eldest son, Minamoto no Yoriyoshi, married Taira no Naokata's daughter and inherited the power of the direct line of the Bando Taira clan who gave birth to his famous grandson [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]]. He was a part of Michinaga’s Four Heavenly Kings along with Fujiwara no Yasumasa, Taira no Korehira and Taira no Muneyori.
The tachi Tenkomaru(天光丸) is said to have been passed down from Tada Mitsunaka to Minamoto no Yorinobu and Minamoto no Yoshiie as a treasured sword of the Genji clan.
His [[:en:Dharma name|Dharma name]] was '''Renshin''' (蓮心). He died on June 1, 1048.
==Family== * Father: ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'' [[:en:Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Minamoto no Mitsunaka]] * Mother: daughter of Fujiwara no Munetada * Wife: Shuri no Myobu * Children: [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * Grandchildren: [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] ** [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna]] ***[[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]]
== See also == * [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] * [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] * [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * [[Hachiman]] * [[:en:Kidōmaru|Kidōmaru]]
==References== {{reflist}} *Sansom, George (1958). 'A History of Japan to 1334'. Stanford, California: Stanford University Press.

{{ill|Fujiwara no Munetada's daughter|WD=Q106326646}}|native_name=源頼信|given_name={{ill|Yorinobu|WD=Q8055328}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2699951]] Wikidata name: Minamoto no Yorinobu [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Founder of the Kawachi Genji)}} [[Image:Minamoto Yorinobu.jpg|thumb|250px|Minamoto no Yorinobu]] {{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} {{Nihongo|'''Minamoto no Yorinobu'''|源 頼信||December 21, 968 – June 1, 1048}} was a {{ill|Samurai|en|Samurai|WD=Q38142|lt=Samurai}} commander and member of the powerful [[Minamoto clan]]. Along with his brother {{ill|Minamoto no Yorimitsu|en|Minamoto no Yorimitsu|WD=Q590523|lt=Yorimitsu}}, Yorinobu served the regents of the [[Fujiwara clan]], taking the violent measures the Fujiwara were themselves unable to take. He held the title, passed down from his father, of ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'', Commander-in-chief of the Defense of the North. He served as Governor of {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise}}, Shinano, Sagami, Mutsu and {{ill|Kai Province|en|Kai Province|WD=Q858076|lt=Kai Province}}s, and was the progenitor of the {{ill|Kawachi Genji|en|Kawachi Genji|WD=Q5360652|lt=Kawachi Genji}}.
== Biography == [[File:Minamotono Yorinobu.jpg|thumb|Minamoto no Yorinobu approaches the residence of Taira no Tadatsune through the inland sea.]] Yorinobu was born on December 21, 968, the son of {{ill|Minamoto no Mitsunaka|en|Minamoto no Mitsunaka|WD=Q2531241|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka}} (912–997).
Yorinobu is particularly known for being a favorite of the [[:en:Sessho and Kampaku|Regent]] {{ill|Fujiwara no Michinaga|en|Fujiwara no Michinaga|WD=Q433133|lt=Fujiwara no Michinaga}}, and for suppressing a revolt by {{ill|Taira no Tadatsune|en|Taira no Tadatsune|WD=Q1105844|lt=Taira no Tadatsune}}, vice-governor of {{ill|Kazusa Province|en|Kazusa Province|WD=Q1055394|lt=Kazusa Province}}. He had originally refused, for personal reasons, but eventually assented to taking on the charge of defeating Tadatsune, after being made Governor of Kai. While making preparations to attack, Tadatsune surrendered, offering no resistance in 1031. In putting an end to this rebellion, Yorinobu not only furthered the Court's goals and his own reputation, but he created an opening for [[Minamoto clan|Minamoto]] influence in the eastern portions of the country.<ref name=Sansom>{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A history of Japan to 1334 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1958 |ISBN=0804705232 |page=247-248}}</ref>
In a statement to Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine, it was stated that the reason they were able to put down the rebellion so successfully was due to the blessing of inheriting the blood of [[Hachiman]] Daibosatsu and [[:en:Emperor Ojin|Emperor Ojin]].
The story of the capture of Tadatsune while Yorinobu was the Governor of Kai, and other stories while he was the governor of {{ill|Kōzuke Province|en|Kōzuke Province|WD=Q1054838|lt=Kōzuke Province}} and {{ill|Hitachi Province|en|Hitachi Province|WD=Q1055353|lt=Hitachi Province}}, appear in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatarishū|en|Konjaku Monogatarishū|WD=Q1507085|lt=Konjaku Monogatarishū}} (vol. 25, sec. 9 and 11).<ref name=Sato>{{Cite book |last=Sato |first=Hiroaki |title=Legends of the Samurai |publisher=Overlook Duckworth |year=1995 |ISBN=9781590207307 |page=71-79}}</ref>
After this rebellion, the warriors of Bando (the Taira clan, the Fujiwara clan of the Hidesato lineage, etc.) began to form vassal relationships with the Kawachi Genji, laying the foundation for their later domination of the eastern provinces and for the mainstreaming of the Minamoto clan as a warrior family.
His eldest son, Minamoto no Yoriyoshi, married Taira no Naokata's daughter and inherited the power of the direct line of the Bando Taira clan who gave birth to his famous grandson {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|WD=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie}}. He was a part of Michinaga’s Four Heavenly Kings along with Fujiwara no Yasumasa, Taira no Korehira and Taira no Muneyori.
The tachi Tenkomaru(天光丸) is said to have been passed down from Tada Mitsunaka to Minamoto no Yorinobu and Minamoto no Yoshiie as a treasured sword of the Genji clan.
His {{ill|Dharma name|en|Dharma name|WD=Q1543672|lt=Dharma name}} was '''Renshin''' (蓮心). He died on June 1, 1048.
==Family== * Father: ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'' {{ill|Minamoto no Mitsunaka|en|Minamoto no Mitsunaka|WD=Q2531241|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka}} * Mother: daughter of Fujiwara no Munetada * Wife: Shuri no Myobu * Children: [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * Grandchildren: {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|WD=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie}} ** {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|en|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|WD=Q11564276|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsuna}} ***{{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|en|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|WD=Q3083118|lt=Minamoto no Yoshimitsu}}
== See also == * [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|WD=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie}} * {{ill|Seiwa Genji|en|Seiwa Genji|WD=Q2164991|lt=Seiwa Genji}} * [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * [[Hachiman]] * {{ill|Kidōmaru|en|Kidōmaru|WD=Q10566902|lt=Kidōmaru}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[fa:میناموتو نو یورینوبو]][[fr:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[it:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[ja:源頼信]][[ko:미나모토노 요리노부]][[pt:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[ru:Минамото-но Ёринобу]][[sv:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[uk:Мінамото но Йорінобу]][[zh:源賴信]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[fa:میناموتو نو یورینوبو]][[fr:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[it:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[ja:源頼信]][[ko:미나모토노 요리노부]][[pt:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[ru:Минамото-но Ёринобу]][[sv:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[uk:Мінамото но Йорінобу]][[zh:源賴信]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[fa:میناموتو نو یورینوبو]][[fr:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[it:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[ja:源頼信]][[ko:미나모토노 요리노부]][[pt:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[ru:Минамото-но Ёринобу]][[sv:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[uk:Мінамото но Йорінобу]][[zh:源賴信]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Minamoto Yorinobu.jpg|birth_date={{birth date|0968|12|21}}|death_date={{death date|1048|6|1}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|military personnel|WD=Q47064}}; {{ill|samurai|WD=Q38142}}|children={{ill|Minamoto no Yorikiyo|WD=Q11564357}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yorinobu's daughter|WD=Q106533657}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yorisue|WD=Q11564346}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q106533719}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|WD=Q3082851}}; {{ill|Q106619424|WD=Q106619424}}|father={{ill|Minamoto no Mitsunaka|WD=Q2531241}}|mother={{ill|Fujiwara no Motokata's daughter (Minamoto no Mitsunaka's wife)|WD=Q106326720}}; {{ill|Fujiwara no Munetada's daughter|WD=Q106326646}}|native_name=源頼信|given_name={{ill|Yorinobu|WD=Q8055328}}}} {{Short description|Founder of the Kawachi Genji)}} [[Image:Minamoto Yorinobu.jpg|thumb|250px|Minamoto no Yorinobu]] {{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} {{Nihongo|'''Minamoto no Yorinobu'''|源 頼信||December 21, 968 – June 1, 1048}} was a {{ill|Samurai|en|Samurai|WD=Q38142|lt=Samurai}} commander and member of the powerful [[Minamoto clan]]. Along with his brother {{ill|Minamoto no Yorimitsu|en|Minamoto no Yorimitsu|WD=Q590523|lt=Yorimitsu}}, Yorinobu served the regents of the [[Fujiwara clan]], taking the violent measures the Fujiwara were themselves unable to take. He held the title, passed down from his father, of ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'', Commander-in-chief of the Defense of the North. He served as Governor of {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise}}, Shinano, Sagami, Mutsu and {{ill|Kai Province|en|Kai Province|WD=Q858076|lt=Kai Province}}s, and was the progenitor of the {{ill|Kawachi Genji|en|Kawachi Genji|WD=Q5360652|lt=Kawachi Genji}}.
== Biography == [[File:Minamotono Yorinobu.jpg|thumb|Minamoto no Yorinobu approaches the residence of Taira no Tadatsune through the inland sea.]] Yorinobu was born on December 21, 968, the son of {{ill|Minamoto no Mitsunaka|en|Minamoto no Mitsunaka|WD=Q2531241|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka}} (912–997).
Yorinobu is particularly known for being a favorite of the [[:en:Sessho and Kampaku|Regent]] {{ill|Fujiwara no Michinaga|en|Fujiwara no Michinaga|WD=Q433133|lt=Fujiwara no Michinaga}}, and for suppressing a revolt by {{ill|Taira no Tadatsune|en|Taira no Tadatsune|WD=Q1105844|lt=Taira no Tadatsune}}, vice-governor of {{ill|Kazusa Province|en|Kazusa Province|WD=Q1055394|lt=Kazusa Province}}. He had originally refused, for personal reasons, but eventually assented to taking on the charge of defeating Tadatsune, after being made Governor of Kai. While making preparations to attack, Tadatsune surrendered, offering no resistance in 1031. In putting an end to this rebellion, Yorinobu not only furthered the Court's goals and his own reputation, but he created an opening for [[Minamoto clan|Minamoto]] influence in the eastern portions of the country.<ref name=Sansom>{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A history of Japan to 1334 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1958 |ISBN=0804705232 |page=247-248}}</ref>
In a statement to Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine, it was stated that the reason they were able to put down the rebellion so successfully was due to the blessing of inheriting the blood of [[Hachiman]] Daibosatsu and [[:en:Emperor Ojin|Emperor Ojin]].
The story of the capture of Tadatsune while Yorinobu was the Governor of Kai, and other stories while he was the governor of {{ill|Kōzuke Province|en|Kōzuke Province|WD=Q1054838|lt=Kōzuke Province}} and {{ill|Hitachi Province|en|Hitachi Province|WD=Q1055353|lt=Hitachi Province}}, appear in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatarishū|en|Konjaku Monogatarishū|WD=Q1507085|lt=Konjaku Monogatarishū}} (vol. 25, sec. 9 and 11).<ref name=Sato>{{Cite book |last=Sato |first=Hiroaki |title=Legends of the Samurai |publisher=Overlook Duckworth |year=1995 |ISBN=9781590207307 |page=71-79}}</ref>
After this rebellion, the warriors of Bando (the Taira clan, the Fujiwara clan of the Hidesato lineage, etc.) began to form vassal relationships with the Kawachi Genji, laying the foundation for their later domination of the eastern provinces and for the mainstreaming of the Minamoto clan as a warrior family.
His eldest son, Minamoto no Yoriyoshi, married Taira no Naokata's daughter and inherited the power of the direct line of the Bando Taira clan who gave birth to his famous grandson {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|WD=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie}}. He was a part of Michinaga’s Four Heavenly Kings along with Fujiwara no Yasumasa, Taira no Korehira and Taira no Muneyori.
The tachi Tenkomaru(天光丸) is said to have been passed down from Tada Mitsunaka to Minamoto no Yorinobu and Minamoto no Yoshiie as a treasured sword of the Genji clan.
His {{ill|Dharma name|en|Dharma name|WD=Q1543672|lt=Dharma name}} was '''Renshin''' (蓮心). He died on June 1, 1048.
==Family== * Father: ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'' {{ill|Minamoto no Mitsunaka|en|Minamoto no Mitsunaka|WD=Q2531241|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka}} * Mother: daughter of Fujiwara no Munetada * Wife: Shuri no Myobu * Children: [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * Grandchildren: {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|WD=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie}} ** {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|en|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|WD=Q11564276|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsuna}} ***{{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|en|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|WD=Q3083118|lt=Minamoto no Yoshimitsu}}
== See also == * [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|WD=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie}} * {{ill|Seiwa Genji|en|Seiwa Genji|WD=Q2164991|lt=Seiwa Genji}} * [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * [[Hachiman]] * {{ill|Kidōmaru|en|Kidōmaru|WD=Q10566902|lt=Kidōmaru}}
==References== {{reflist}} *Sansom, George (1958). 'A History of Japan to 1334'. Stanford, California: Stanford University Press.

The story of the capture of Tadatsune while Yorinobu was the Governor of Kai, and other stories while he was the governor of {{ill|Kōzuke Province|en|Kōzuke Province|WD=Q1054838|lt=Kōzuke Province}} and {{ill|Hitachi Province|en|Hitachi Province|WD=Q1055353|lt=Hitachi Province}}, appear in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatarishū|en|Konjaku Monogatarishū|WD=Q1507085|lt=Konjaku Monogatarishū}} (vol. 25, sec. 9 and 11).<ref name=Sato>{{Cite book |last=Sato |first=Hiroaki |title=Legends of the Samurai |publisher=Overlook Duckworth |year=1995 |ISBN=9781590207307 |page=71-79}}</ref>
After this rebellion, the warriors of Bando (the Taira clan, the Fujiwara clan of the Hidesato lineage, etc.) began to form vassal relationships with the Kawachi Genji, laying the foundation for their later domination of the eastern provinces and for the mainstreaming of the Minamoto clan as a warrior family.
His eldest son, Minamoto no Yoriyoshi, married Taira no Naokata's daughter and inherited the power of the direct line of the Bando Taira clan who gave birth to his famous grandson {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|WD=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie}}. He was a part of Michinaga’s Four Heavenly Kings along with Fujiwara no Yasumasa, Taira no Korehira and Taira no Muneyori.
The tachi Tenkomaru(天光丸) is said to have been passed down from Tada Mitsunaka to Minamoto no Yorinobu and Minamoto no Yoshiie as a treasured sword of the Genji clan.
His {{ill|Dharma name|en|Dharma name|WD=Q1543672|lt=Dharma name}} was '''Renshin''' (蓮心). He died on June 1, 1048.
==Family== * Father: ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'' {{ill|Minamoto no Mitsunaka|en|Minamoto no Mitsunaka|WD=Q2531241|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka}} * Mother: daughter of Fujiwara no Munetada * Wife: Shuri no Myobu * Children: [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * Grandchildren: {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|WD=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie}} ** {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|en|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|WD=Q11564276|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsuna}} ***{{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|en|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|WD=Q3083118|lt=Minamoto no Yoshimitsu}}

Mother of Fujiwara no Sukekazu}}|father={{ill|Minamoto no Mitsunaka|WD=Q2531241|1=Minamoto no Mitsunaka}}|mother={{ill|Fujiwara no Motokata's daughter (Minamoto no Mitsunaka's wife)|WD=Q106326720|1=Fujiwara no Motokata's daughter (Minamoto no Mitsunaka's wife)}}; {{ill|Fujiwara no Munetada's daughter|WD=Q106326646|1=Fujiwara no Munetada's daughter}}|native_name=源頼信|given_name={{ill|Yorinobu|WD=Q8055328|1=Yorinobu}}}} {{Short description|Founder of the Kawachi Genji)}} [[Image:Minamoto Yorinobu.jpg|thumb|250px|Minamoto no Yorinobu]] {{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} {{Nihongo|'''Minamoto no Yorinobu'''|源 頼信||December 21, 968 – June 1, 1048}} was a {{ill|Samurai|en|Samurai|WD=Q38142|lt=Samurai|1=Samurai}} commander and member of the powerful [[Minamoto clan]]. Along with his brother {{ill|Minamoto no Yorimitsu|en|Minamoto no Yorimitsu|WD=Q590523|lt=Yorimitsu|1=Minamoto no Yorimitsu}}, Yorinobu served the regents of the [[Fujiwara clan]], taking the violent measures the Fujiwara were themselves unable to take. He held the title, passed down from his father, of ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'', Commander-in-chief of the Defense of the North. He served as Governor of {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise|1=Ise Province}}, Shinano, Sagami, Mutsu and {{ill|Kai Province|en|Kai Province|WD=Q858076|lt=Kai Province|1=Kai Province}}s, and was the progenitor of the {{ill|Kawachi Genji|en|Kawachi Genji|WD=Q5360652|lt=Kawachi Genji|1=Kawachi Genji}}.
== Biography == [[File:Minamotono Yorinobu.jpg|thumb|Minamoto no Yorinobu approaches the residence of Taira no Tadatsune through the inland sea.]] Yorinobu was born on December 21, 968, the son of {{ill|Minamoto no Mitsunaka|en|Minamoto no Mitsunaka|WD=Q2531241|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka|1=Minamoto no Mitsunaka}} (912–997).

He had originally refused, for personal reasons, but eventually assented to taking on the charge of defeating Tadatsune, after being made Governor of Kai. While making preparations to attack, Tadatsune surrendered, offering no resistance in 1031. In putting an end to this rebellion, Yorinobu not only furthered the Court's goals and his own reputation, but he created an opening for [[Minamoto clan|Minamoto]] influence in the eastern portions of the country.<ref name=Sansom>{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A history of Japan to 1334 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1958 |ISBN=0804705232 |page=247-248}}</ref>
In a statement to Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine, it was stated that the reason they were able to put down the rebellion so successfully was due to the blessing of inheriting the blood of [[Hachiman]] Daibosatsu and [[:en:Emperor Ojin|Emperor Ojin]].

After this rebellion, the warriors of Bando (the Taira clan, the Fujiwara clan of the Hidesato lineage, etc.) began to form vassal relationships with the Kawachi Genji, laying the foundation for their later domination of the eastern provinces and for the mainstreaming of the Minamoto clan as a warrior family.
His eldest son, Minamoto no Yoriyoshi, married Taira no Naokata's daughter and inherited the power of the direct line of the Bando Taira clan who gave birth to his famous grandson {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|WD=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie}}. The tachi Tenkomaru(天光丸) is said to have been passed down from Tada Mitsunaka to Minamoto no Yorinobu and Minamoto no Yoshiie as a treasured sword of the Genji clan.
His {{ill|Dharma name|en|Dharma name|WD=Q1543672|lt=Dharma name|1=Dharma name}} was '''Renshin''' (蓮心). He died on June 1, 1048.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[fa:میناموتو نو یورینوبو]][[fr:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[it:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[ja:源頼信]][[ko:미나모토노 요리노부]][[pt:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[ru:Минамото-но Ёринобу]][[sv:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[uk:Мінамото но Йорінобу]][[zh:源賴信]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[fa:میناموتو نو یورینوبو]][[fr:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[it:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[ja:源頼信]][[ko:미나모토노 요리노부]][[pt:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[ru:Минамото-но Ёринобу]][[sv:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[uk:Мінамото но Йорінобу]][[zh:源賴信]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[fa:میناموتو نو یورینوبو]][[fr:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[it:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[ja:源頼信]][[ko:미나모토노 요리노부]][[pt:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[ru:Минамото-но Ёринобу]][[sv:Minamoto no Yorinobu]][[uk:Мінамото но Йорінобу]][[zh:源賴信]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Minamoto Yorinobu.jpg|birth_date={{birth date|0968|12|21}}|death_date={{death date|1048|6|1}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17|1=Japan}}|occupation={{ill|military personnel|WD=Q47064|1=Military personnel}}; {{ill|samurai|WD=Q38142|1=Samurai}}|children={{ill|Minamoto no Yorikiyo|WD=Q11564357|1=Minamoto no Yorikiyo}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yorinobu's daughter|WD=Q106533657|1=Minamoto no Yorinobu's daughter}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yorisue|WD=Q11564346|1=Minamoto no Yorisue}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q106533719|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|WD=Q3082851|1=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi}}; {{ill|Q106619424|WD=Q106619424|1=Daughter of Minamoto no Yoriyoshi? Mother of Fujiwara no Sukekazu}}|father={{ill|Minamoto no Mitsunaka|WD=Q2531241|1=Minamoto no Mitsunaka}}|mother={{ill|Fujiwara no Motokata's daughter (Minamoto no Mitsunaka's wife)|WD=Q106326720|1=Fujiwara no Motokata's daughter (Minamoto no Mitsunaka's wife)}}; {{ill|Fujiwara no Munetada's daughter|WD=Q106326646|1=Fujiwara no Munetada's daughter}}|native_name=源頼信|given_name={{ill|Yorinobu|WD=Q8055328|1=Yorinobu}}}} {{Short description|Founder of the Kawachi Genji)}} [[Image:Minamoto Yorinobu.jpg|thumb|250px|Minamoto no Yorinobu]] {{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} {{Nihongo|'''Minamoto no Yorinobu'''|源 頼信||December 21, 968 – June 1, 1048}} was a {{ill|Samurai|en|Samurai|WD=Q38142|lt=Samurai|1=Samurai}} commander and member of the powerful [[Minamoto clan]]. Along with his brother {{ill|Minamoto no Yorimitsu|en|Minamoto no Yorimitsu|WD=Q590523|lt=Yorimitsu|1=Minamoto no Yorimitsu}}, Yorinobu served the regents of the [[Fujiwara clan]], taking the violent measures the Fujiwara were themselves unable to take. He held the title, passed down from his father, of ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'', Commander-in-chief of the Defense of the North. He served as Governor of {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise|1=Ise Province}}, Shinano, Sagami, Mutsu and {{ill|Kai Province|en|Kai Province|WD=Q858076|lt=Kai Province|1=Kai Province}}s, and was the progenitor of the {{ill|Kawachi Genji|en|Kawachi Genji|WD=Q5360652|lt=Kawachi Genji|1=Kawachi Genji}}.
== Biography == [[File:Minamotono Yorinobu.jpg|thumb|Minamoto no Yorinobu approaches the residence of Taira no Tadatsune through the inland sea.]] Yorinobu was born on December 21, 968, the son of {{ill|Minamoto no Mitsunaka|en|Minamoto no Mitsunaka|WD=Q2531241|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka|1=Minamoto no Mitsunaka}} (912–997).
Yorinobu is particularly known for being a favorite of the [[:en:Sessho and Kampaku|Regent]] {{ill|Fujiwara no Michinaga|en|Fujiwara no Michinaga|WD=Q433133|lt=Fujiwara no Michinaga|1=Fujiwara no Michinaga}}, and for suppressing a revolt by {{ill|Taira no Tadatsune|en|Taira no Tadatsune|WD=Q1105844|lt=Taira no Tadatsune|1=Taira no Tadatsune}}, vice-governor of {{ill|Kazusa Province|en|Kazusa Province|WD=Q1055394|lt=Kazusa Province|1=Kazusa Province}}. He had originally refused, for personal reasons, but eventually assented to taking on the charge of defeating Tadatsune, after being made Governor of Kai. While making preparations to attack, Tadatsune surrendered, offering no resistance in 1031. In putting an end to this rebellion, Yorinobu not only furthered the Court's goals and his own reputation, but he created an opening for [[Minamoto clan|Minamoto]] influence in the eastern portions of the country.<ref name=Sansom>{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A history of Japan to 1334 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1958 |ISBN=0804705232 |page=247-248}}</ref>
In a statement to Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine, it was stated that the reason they were able to put down the rebellion so successfully was due to the blessing of inheriting the blood of [[Hachiman]] Daibosatsu and [[:en:Emperor Ojin|Emperor Ojin]].
The story of the capture of Tadatsune while Yorinobu was the Governor of Kai, and other stories while he was the governor of {{ill|Kōzuke Province|en|Kōzuke Province|WD=Q1054838|lt=Kōzuke Province|1=Kōzuke Province}} and {{ill|Hitachi Province|en|Hitachi Province|WD=Q1055353|lt=Hitachi Province|1=Hitachi Province}}, appear in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatarishū|en|Konjaku Monogatarishū|WD=Q1507085|lt=Konjaku Monogatarishū|1=Konjaku Monogatarishū}} (vol. 25, sec. 9 and 11).<ref name=Sato>{{Cite book |last=Sato |first=Hiroaki |title=Legends of the Samurai |publisher=Overlook Duckworth |year=1995 |ISBN=9781590207307 |page=71-79}}</ref>
After this rebellion, the warriors of Bando (the Taira clan, the Fujiwara clan of the Hidesato lineage, etc.) began to form vassal relationships with the Kawachi Genji, laying the foundation for their later domination of the eastern provinces and for the mainstreaming of the Minamoto clan as a warrior family.
His eldest son, Minamoto no Yoriyoshi, married Taira no Naokata's daughter and inherited the power of the direct line of the Bando Taira clan who gave birth to his famous grandson {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|WD=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie}}. He was a part of Michinaga’s Four Heavenly Kings along with Fujiwara no Yasumasa, Taira no Korehira and Taira no Muneyori.
The tachi Tenkomaru(天光丸) is said to have been passed down from Tada Mitsunaka to Minamoto no Yorinobu and Minamoto no Yoshiie as a treasured sword of the Genji clan.
His {{ill|Dharma name|en|Dharma name|WD=Q1543672|lt=Dharma name|1=Dharma name}} was '''Renshin''' (蓮心). He died on June 1, 1048.
==Family== * Father: ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'' {{ill|Minamoto no Mitsunaka|en|Minamoto no Mitsunaka|WD=Q2531241|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka|1=Minamoto no Mitsunaka}} * Mother: daughter of Fujiwara no Munetada * Wife: Shuri no Myobu * Children: [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * Grandchildren: {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|WD=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie}} ** {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|en|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|WD=Q11564276|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsuna}} ***{{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|en|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|WD=Q3083118|lt=Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|1=Minamoto no Yoshimitsu}}
== See also == * [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|WD=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie}} * {{ill|Seiwa Genji|en|Seiwa Genji|WD=Q2164991|lt=Seiwa Genji|1=Seiwa Genji}} * [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * [[Hachiman]] * {{ill|Kidōmaru|en|Kidōmaru|WD=Q10566902|lt=Kidōmaru|1=Kidōmaru}}
==References== {{reflist}} *Sansom, George (1958). 'A History of Japan to 1334'. Stanford, California: Stanford University Press.

He served as Governor of {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|qid=Q864166|lt=Ise|1=Ise Province}}, Shinano, Sagami, Mutsu and {{ill|Kai Province|en|Kai Province|qid=Q858076|lt=Kai Province|1=Kai Province}}s, and was the progenitor of the {{ill|Kawachi Genji|en|Kawachi Genji|qid=Q5360652|lt=Kawachi Genji|1=Kawachi Genji}}.
== Biography == [[File:Minamotono Yorinobu.jpg|thumb|Minamoto no Yorinobu approaches the residence of Taira no Tadatsune through the inland sea.]] Yorinobu was born on December 21, 968, the son of {{ill|Minamoto no Mitsunaka|en|Minamoto no Mitsunaka|qid=Q2531241|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka|1=Minamoto no Mitsunaka}} (912–997).
Yorinobu is particularly known for being a favorite of the [[:en:Sessho and Kampaku|Regent]] {{ill|Fujiwara no Michinaga|en|Fujiwara no Michinaga|qid=Q433133|lt=Fujiwara no Michinaga|1=Fujiwara no Michinaga}}, and for suppressing a revolt by {{ill|Taira no Tadatsune|en|Taira no Tadatsune|qid=Q1105844|lt=Taira no Tadatsune|1=Taira no Tadatsune}}, vice-governor of {{ill|Kazusa Province|en|Kazusa Province|qid=Q1055394|lt=Kazusa Province|1=Kazusa Province}}. He had originally refused, for personal reasons, but eventually assented to taking on the charge of defeating Tadatsune, after being made Governor of Kai. While making preparations to attack, Tadatsune surrendered, offering no resistance in 1031. In putting an end to this rebellion, Yorinobu not only furthered the Court's goals and his own reputation, but he created an opening for [[Minamoto clan|Minamoto]] influence in the eastern portions of the country.<ref name=Sansom>{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A history of Japan to 1334 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1958 |ISBN=0804705232 |page=247-248}}</ref>
In a statement to Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine, it was stated that the reason they were able to put down the rebellion so successfully was due to the blessing of inheriting the blood of [[Hachiman]] Daibosatsu and [[:en:Emperor Ojin|Emperor Ojin]].
The story of the capture of Tadatsune while Yorinobu was the Governor of Kai, and other stories while he was the governor of {{ill|Kōzuke Province|en|Kōzuke Province|qid=Q1054838|lt=Kōzuke Province|1=Kōzuke Province}} and {{ill|Hitachi Province|en|Hitachi Province|qid=Q1055353|lt=Hitachi Province|1=Hitachi Province}}, appear in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatarishū|en|Konjaku Monogatarishū|qid=Q1507085|lt=Konjaku Monogatarishū|1=Konjaku Monogatarishū}} (vol. 25, sec. 9 and 11).<ref name=Sato>{{Cite book |last=Sato |first=Hiroaki |title=Legends of the Samurai |publisher=Overlook Duckworth |year=1995 |ISBN=9781590207307 |page=71-79}}</ref>
After this rebellion, the warriors of Bando (the Taira clan, the Fujiwara clan of the Hidesato lineage, etc.) began to form vassal relationships with the Kawachi Genji, laying the foundation for their later domination of the eastern provinces and for the mainstreaming of the Minamoto clan as a warrior family.
His eldest son, Minamoto no Yoriyoshi, married Taira no Naokata's daughter and inherited the power of the direct line of the Bando Taira clan who gave birth to his famous grandson {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|qid=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie}}. He was a part of Michinaga’s Four Heavenly Kings along with Fujiwara no Yasumasa, Taira no Korehira and Taira no Muneyori.
The tachi Tenkomaru(天光丸) is said to have been passed down from Tada Mitsunaka to Minamoto no Yorinobu and Minamoto no Yoshiie as a treasured sword of the Genji clan.
His {{ill|Dharma name|en|Dharma name|qid=Q1543672|lt=Dharma name|1=Dharma name}} was '''Renshin''' (蓮心). He died on June 1, 1048.
==Family== * Father: ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'' {{ill|Minamoto no Mitsunaka|en|Minamoto no Mitsunaka|qid=Q2531241|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka|1=Minamoto no Mitsunaka}} * Mother: daughter of Fujiwara no Munetada * Wife: Shuri no Myobu * Children: [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * Grandchildren: {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|qid=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie}} ** {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|en|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|qid=Q11564276|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsuna}} ***{{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|en|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|qid=Q3083118|lt=Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|1=Minamoto no Yoshimitsu}}
== See also == * [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|qid=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie}} * {{ill|Seiwa Genji|en|Seiwa Genji|qid=Q2164991|lt=Seiwa Genji|1=Seiwa Genji}} * [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * [[Hachiman]] * {{ill|Kidōmaru|en|Kidōmaru|qid=Q10566902|lt=Kidōmaru|1=Kidōmaru}}
